mnemonics.title: P-P-P Memory Rule
mnemonics.content: Public in Base → Protected in derived class when using Protected inheritance: P-P-P = Private becomes Private, Public becomes Protected, Protected becomes Private

mnemonics.title: Constructor Order Song
mnemonics.content: Base first, then derived; Members next, class last; Destructors reverse to make the order fast

mnemonics.title: Diamond Solution
mnemonics.content: To solve Diamond Problem: VIRTUAL is the key - make base class virtual in intermediate classes

acronyms.usage: Paradigm that uses inheritance as one of its pillars
Inheritance represents IS-A relationship: 'Dog IS-A Animal'

visualTips.visualization: Draw an inverted tree with root at top (base class) and branches going down (derived classes). Use arrows pointing from derived to base.
Use a 3x3 matrix: Rows = Base class access, Columns = Inheritance type. Cells show resulting access in derived class.
Draw a diamond shape: Top = CommonBase, Left = Derived1, Right = Derived2, Bottom = MostDerived. Without virtual, Bottom has two paths to Top.
Draw a timeline: Base constructor → Member constructors → Derived constructor → (reverse for destructors)

analogies.analogy: Like genetic inheritance in families - children inherit traits (attributes) and abilities (methods) from parents, but can also have their own unique traits.
Like family heirlooms - not publicly accessible to strangers (private), but family members (derived classes) can access them, unlike completely private items.
Like a shared grandparent in a family tree - without virtual, grandparents appear twice; with virtual, there's only one copy shared by all.
Like a child who inherits traits from both mother and father - has characteristics from both parent classes, but may have conflicts (ambiguity) if both parents have same name traits.
